title: 'Once-in-a-millennium' asteroid flyby will be visible to much of the world in 2029 - Space
summary_full: 'Once-in-a-millennium' asteroid flyby will be visible to much of the world in 2029  Space80 million trees were wiped out in seconds in Siberia after a space rock explosion more powerful than the Hiroshima bomb, but scientists never found a crater  The Times of IndiaAsteroid Day & eight other key dates in asteroid history  European Space AgencySkyscraper-sized asteroid 99942 Apophis will pass Earth April 13, 2029  NewsBytes#SpaceWatchGL Opinion: Apophis Is Coming. Planetary Defence Is Coming of Age.  SpaceWatch.GLOBAL
